# Vue & Node admin panel framework

> Launch robust back-office apps faster with AdminForth’s easy setup and customization

This file contains all documentation content in a single document following the llmstxt.org standard.

## GroupBy


Helper to build grouping rules for use with resource().aggregate()

## Constructors

### Constructor

> **new GroupBy**(): `GroupBy`

#### Returns

`GroupBy`

## Methods

### DateTrunc()

> `static` **DateTrunc**(`field`, `truncation`, `timezone?`): [`IGroupByDateTrunc`](../interfaces/IGroupByDateTrunc.md)

Group by date truncation. Returns ISO date strings (YYYY-MM-DD) for each group.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `field` | `string` | Column name (must be DATETIME/DATE type in the resource) |
| `truncation` | `"day"` \| `"week"` \| `"month"` \| `"year"` | 'day' | 'week' | 'month' | 'year' |
| `timezone?` | `string` | IANA timezone name, e.g. 'Europe/Kyiv'. Defaults to 'UTC' when omitted. |

#### Returns

[`IGroupByDateTrunc`](../interfaces/IGroupByDateTrunc.md)

***

### Field()

> `static` **Field**(`field`): [`IGroupByField`](../interfaces/IGroupByField.md)

Group by raw field value. The field value is returned as-is in the `group` key.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `field` | `string` | Column name to group by |

#### Returns

[`IGroupByField`](../interfaces/IGroupByField.md)

---

## Type Aliases

| Type Alias | Description |
| ------ | ------ |
| [AdminForthDataSource](type-aliases/AdminForthDataSource.md) | Data source describes database connection which will be used to fetch data for resources. Each resource should use one data source. |
| [AdminForthExpressSchemaInput](type-aliases/AdminForthExpressSchemaInput.md) | - |
| [AdminUserAuthorizeFunction](type-aliases/AdminUserAuthorizeFunction.md) | Allow to make extra authorization |
| [AfterCreateSaveFunction](type-aliases/AfterCreateSaveFunction.md) | - |
| [AfterDataSourceResponseFunction](type-aliases/AfterDataSourceResponseFunction.md) | Modify response to change how data is returned after fetching from database. Return ok: false and error: string to stop execution and show error message to user. Return ok: true to continue execution. |
| [AfterDeleteSaveFunction](type-aliases/AfterDeleteSaveFunction.md) | Modify record to change how data is saved to database. Return ok: false and error: string to stop execution and show error message to user. Return ok: true to continue execution. |
| [AfterEditSaveFunction](type-aliases/AfterEditSaveFunction.md) | - |
| [AllowedActions](type-aliases/AllowedActions.md) | - |
| [AllowedActionsInput](type-aliases/AllowedActionsInput.md) | Object which describes allowed actions for user. |
| [AllowedActionValue](type-aliases/AllowedActionValue.md) | Defines whether user has access to an action, can statically be Boolean or function which returns Boolean or string with error message |
| [BackendOnlyInput](type-aliases/BackendOnlyInput.md) | - |
| [BeforeCreateSaveFunction](type-aliases/BeforeCreateSaveFunction.md) | - |
| [BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction](type-aliases/BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction.md) | Modify query to change how data is fetched from database. Return ok: false and error: string to stop execution and show error message to user. Return ok: true to continue execution. |
| [BeforeDeleteSaveFunction](type-aliases/BeforeDeleteSaveFunction.md) | Modify record to change how data is saved to database. Return ok: false and error: string to stop execution and show error message to user. Return ok: true to continue execution. |
| [BeforeEditSaveFunction](type-aliases/BeforeEditSaveFunction.md) | - |
| [BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction](type-aliases/BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction.md) | Allow to get user data before login confirmation, will triger when user try to login. |

## AdminForthActionInput


## Properties

### action?

> `optional` **action?**: (`params`) => `Promise`\<\{ `error?`: `string`; `message?`: `string`; `ok`: `boolean`; \}\>

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `params` | \{ `adminforth`: [`IAdminForth`](IAdminForth.md); `adminUser`: [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md); `extra?`: [`HttpExtra`](HttpExtra.md); `recordId`: `string`; `resource`: [`AdminForthResource`](AdminForthResource.md); `response`: [`IAdminForthHttpResponse`](IAdminForthHttpResponse.md); `tr`: [`ITranslateFunction`](ITranslateFunction.md); \} |
| `params.adminforth` | [`IAdminForth`](IAdminForth.md) |
| `params.adminUser` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) |
| `params.extra?` | [`HttpExtra`](HttpExtra.md) |
| `params.recordId` | `string` |
| `params.resource` | [`AdminForthResource`](AdminForthResource.md) |
| `params.response` | [`IAdminForthHttpResponse`](IAdminForthHttpResponse.md) |
| `params.tr` | [`ITranslateFunction`](ITranslateFunction.md) |

## AdminForthBulkAction


## Extends

- [`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md)

## Properties

### action

> **action**: (`__namedParameters`) => `Promise`\<\{ `error?`: `string`; `ok`: `boolean`; `successMessage?`: `string`; \}\>

Callback which will be called on backend when user clicks on action button.
It should return Promise which will be resolved when action is done.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `__namedParameters` | \{ `adminUser`: [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md); `resource`: [`AdminForthResource`](AdminForthResource.md); `response`: [`IAdminForthHttpResponse`](IAdminForthHttpResponse.md); `selectedIds`: `any`[]; `tr`: [`ITranslateFunction`](ITranslateFunction.md); \} |
| `__namedParameters.adminUser` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) |
| `__namedParameters.resource` | [`AdminForthResource`](AdminForthResource.md) |
| `__namedParameters.response` | [`IAdminForthHttpResponse`](IAdminForthHttpResponse.md) |
| `__namedParameters.selectedIds` | `any`[] |
| `__namedParameters.tr` | [`ITranslateFunction`](ITranslateFunction.md) |

#### Returns

`Promise`\<\{ `error?`: `string`; `ok`: `boolean`; `successMessage?`: `string`; \}\>

***

### allowed?

> `optional` **allowed?**: (`__namedParameters`) => `Promise`\<`boolean`\>

Allowed callback called to check whether action is allowed for user.
1. It called first time when user goes to list view. If callback returns false, action button will be hidden on list view.
2. This same callback called second time when user clicks an action button. If callback returns false, action will not be executed.
In second time selectedIds will be passed to callback (because checkbox for items are selected), so you can use this to make additional 
checks ( for example to check if user has permission for certain records ).

Example:

```ts
allowed: async ({ resource, adminUser, selectedIds }) => {
  if (adminUser.dbUser.role !== 'superadmin') {
   return false;
  } 
  return true;
}
```

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `__namedParameters` | \{ `adminUser`: [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md); `allowedActions`: [`AllowedActionsResolved`](../../Common/type-aliases/AllowedActionsResolved.md); `resource`: [`AdminForthResource`](AdminForthResource.md); `selectedIds?`: `any`[]; \} | - |
| `__namedParameters.adminUser` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) | Admin user object |
| `__namedParameters.allowedActions` | [`AllowedActionsResolved`](../../Common/type-aliases/AllowedActionsResolved.md) | Allowed standard actions for current user resolved by calling allowedActions callbacks if they are passed. You can use this variable to rely on standard actions permissions. E.g. if you have custom actions "Mark as read", you might want to allow it only for users who have "edit" action allowed: Example: `options: \{ bulkActions: [ \{ label: 'Mark as read', action: async (\{ resource, recordIds \}) => \{ await markAsRead(recordIds); \}, allowed: (\{ allowedActions \}) => allowedActions.edit, \} ], allowedActions: \{ edit: (\{ resource, adminUser, recordIds \}) => \{ return adminUser.dbUser.role === 'superadmin'; \} \} \}` |
| `__namedParameters.resource` | [`AdminForthResource`](AdminForthResource.md) | - |
| `__namedParameters.selectedIds?` | `any`[] | recordIds will be passed only once user tries to perform bulk action by clicking on button |

#### Returns

`Promise`\<`boolean`\>

***

### badge?

> `optional` **badge?**: `string`

Optional small badge for button which will be displayed in the list view

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md).[`badge`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md#badge)

***

### buttonCustomCssClass?

> `optional` **buttonCustomCssClass?**: `string`

Add custom class

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md).[`buttonCustomCssClass`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md#buttoncustomcssclass)

***

### confirm?

> `optional` **confirm?**: `string`

Confirmation message which will be displayed to user before action is executed.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md).[`confirm`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md#confirm)

***

### icon?

> `optional` **icon?**: `string`

Icon for action button which will be displayed in the list view

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md).[`icon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md#icon)

***

### id?

> `optional` **id?**: `string`

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md).[`id`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md#id)

***

### label

> **label**: `string`

Label for action button which will be displayed in the list view

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md).[`label`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md#label)

***

### showInThreeDotsDropdown?

> `optional` **showInThreeDotsDropdown?**: `boolean`

Show in three dots dropdown menu in list view.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md).[`showInThreeDotsDropdown`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md#showinthreedotsdropdown)

***

### successMessage?

> `optional` **successMessage?**: `string`

Success message which will be displayed to user after action is executed.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthBulkActionCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md).[`successMessage`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthBulkActionCommon.md#successmessage)

## AdminForthConfig


## Extends

- `Omit`\<[`AdminForthInputConfig`](AdminForthInputConfig.md), `"customization"` \| `"resources"`\>

## Properties

### auth?

> `optional` **auth?**: `object`

Authorization module configuration

#### adminUserAuthorize?

> `optional` **adminUserAuthorize?**: [`AdminUserAuthorizeFunction`](../type-aliases/AdminUserAuthorizeFunction.md) \| [`AdminUserAuthorizeFunction`](../type-aliases/AdminUserAuthorizeFunction.md)[]

Array of functions which will be called before any request to AdminForth API.

#### avatarUrl?

> `optional` **avatarUrl?**: (`adminUser`) => `string` \| `Promise`\<`string`\> \| `Promise`\<`undefined`\>

Function to return avatar URL for user.

##### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `adminUser` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) |

##### Returns

`string` \| `Promise`\<`string`\> \| `Promise`\<`undefined`\>

#### beforeLoginConfirmation?

> `optional` **beforeLoginConfirmation?**: [`BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction.md) \| [`BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction.md)[]

Function or functions  which will be called before user try to login.
Each function will resive User object as an argument

#### clientIpHeader?

> `optional` **clientIpHeader?**: `string`

Client IP header name. If set, AdminForth will use this header to get client IP address.
Otherwise it will use first IP address from X-Forwarded-For header.
If you are using Cloudflare, set this to 'CF-Connecting-IP'. Case-insensitive.

#### demoCredentials?

> `optional` **demoCredentials?**: `string`

Pair of login and pass substitution for demo mode. Split by ':'
! This option is for demo purposes only, never use it for your projects

#### loginBackgroundImage?

> `optional` **loginBackgroundImage?**: `string`

File path to custom background image for login page
Example:
Place file `login-background.jpg` to `./custom` folder and set this option:

```ts
loginBackgroundImage: '@@/login-background.jpg',
```

#### loginBackgroundPosition?

> `optional` **loginBackgroundPosition?**: `"over"` \| `"1/2"` \| `"1/3"` \| `"2/3"` \| `"3/4"` \| `"2/5"` \| `"3/5"`

Position of background image on login page
'over' - image will be displayed over full login page under login form
'1/2' - image will be displayed on left 1/2 of login page

Default: '1/2'

#### loginPromptHTML?

> `optional` **loginPromptHTML?**: `string` \| (() => `string` \| `void` \| `Promise`\<`string` \| `void`\>)

Any prompt to show users on login. Supports HTML.

#### passwordHashField

> **passwordHashField**: `string`

Field name (column name) in user resource which will be used to get hash of password.
Can be e.g. 'passwordHash'

#### ~~rememberMeDays?~~

> `optional` **rememberMeDays?**: `number`

Old variable for remember me duration. Use rememberMeDuration instead.

##### Deprecated

Use rememberMeDuration instead.

#### rememberMeDuration?

> `optional` **rememberMeDuration?**: `string`

Remember me duration for "Remember Me" checkbox on login page.
If not set or set to null/undefined, "Remember Me" checkbox will not be displayed.
If rememberMeDuration is set, then users who check "Remember Me" will be staying logged in for this amount of time.
Format: "1s" (seconds), "1m" (minutes), "1h" (hours), or "1d" (days).
Example: "30d" for 30 days, "7d" for 7 days, "24h" for 24 hours.

#### removeBackgroundBlendMode?

> `optional` **removeBackgroundBlendMode?**: `boolean`

If true, background blend mode will be removed from login background image when position is 'over'

Default: false

#### resourceId?

> `optional` **resourceId?**: `string`

Legacy field left for backward compatibility. Use usersResourceId instead.

#### userFullNameField?

> `optional` **userFullNameField?**: `string`

Optionally if your users table has a field(column) with full name, you can set it here.
This field will be used to display user name in the top right corner of the admin panel.

#### userMenuSettingsPages?

> `optional` **userMenuSettingsPages?**: `object`[]

Add custom page to the settings page

#### usernameField

> **usernameField**: `string`

Field name (column name) in user resource which will be used as username for searching user in database during login.
Can be e.g. 'email' or 'username'

#### usersResourceId?

> `optional` **usersResourceId?**: `string`

Resource ID for resource which stores user table.
Resource is a table in database where users will be stored and fetched from. Resources and their ids are defined in resources section of the config.
In other words this setting is a reference to a table in database where users will be fetched from on login.

#### websocketSubscribed?

> `optional` **websocketSubscribed?**: (`topic`, `user`) => `void`

callback which will be called after user subscribes to websocket topic

##### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `topic` | `string` | topic on which user subscribed |
| `user` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) | user object |

##### Returns

`void`

#### websocketTopicAuth?

> `optional` **websocketTopicAuth?**: (`topic`, `user`) => `Promise`\<`boolean`\>

Can be used to limit user access when subscribing from frontend to websocket topics.

##### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `topic` | `string` | topic where user is trying to subscribe |
| `user` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) | user object |

##### Returns

`Promise`\<`boolean`\>

- boolean, true if user is allowed to subscribe to this topic, false otherwise

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthInputConfig`](AdminForthInputConfig.md).[`auth`](AdminForthInputConfig.md#auth)

***

### baseUrl

> **baseUrl**: `string`

If you want to Serve AdminForth from a subdirectory, e.g. on example.com/backoffice, you can specify it like:

```ts
baseUrl: '/backoffice',
```

#### Overrides

[`AdminForthInputConfig`](AdminForthInputConfig.md).[`baseUrl`](AdminForthInputConfig.md#baseurl)

***

### baseUrlSlashed

> **baseUrlSlashed**: `string`

***

### componentsToExplicitRegister?

> `optional` **componentsToExplicitRegister?**: [`AdminForthComponentDeclarationFull`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthComponentDeclarationFull.md)[]

Most of components are explicitely registered in AdminForth e.g. when you are using them in renderers, page injections and so on.
But some times you might want to have some components registered globally Explicitly. E.g. for ussage in other components without import.

```ts
componentsToExplicitRegister: [
 {
   file: '@@/my-component.vue',
   meta: {
     some: 'meta'
   }
 }
```

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthInputConfig`](AdminForthInputConfig.md).[`componentsToExplicitRegister`](AdminForthInputConfig.md#componentstoexplicitregister)

***

### customization

> **customization**: [`AdminForthConfigCustomization`](AdminForthConfigCustomization.md)

***

### databaseConnectors?

> `optional` **databaseConnectors?**: `object`

If you want use custom DataSource which is not supported by AdminForth yet, you can define it's class here

#### Index Signature

\[`key`: `string`\]: [`IAdminForthDataSourceConnectorConstructor`](IAdminForthDataSourceConnectorConstructor.md)

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthInputConfig`](AdminForthInputConfig.md).[`databaseConnectors`](AdminForthInputConfig.md#databaseconnectors)

***

### dataSources

> **dataSources**: [`AdminForthDataSource`](../type-aliases/AdminForthDataSource.md)[]

List of data sources which will be used to fetch data for resources.
Datasource is one database connection

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthInputConfig`](AdminForthInputConfig.md).[`dataSources`](AdminForthInputConfig.md#datasources)

***

### menu

> **menu**: [`AdminForthConfigMenuItem`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthConfigMenuItem.md)[]

Array of left sidebar menu items which will be displayed in the admin panel.
Menu items can be links to resources or custom pages.
Menu items can be grouped.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthInputConfig`](AdminForthInputConfig.md).[`menu`](AdminForthInputConfig.md#menu)

***

### resources

> **resources**: [`AdminForthResource`](AdminForthResource.md)[]

---

## AdminForthConfigCustomization


## Extends

- `Omit`\<`AdminForthInputConfigCustomization`, `"loginPageInjections"` \| `"globalInjections"`\>

## Properties

### announcementBadge?

> `optional` **announcementBadge?**: (`user`) => [`AnnouncementBadgeResponse`](../../Common/type-aliases/AnnouncementBadgeResponse.md)

Function to return custom badge in side bar for users. Can return text or html
If function is not passed or returns null, badge will not be shown.
Execution is done on admin app load.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `user` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) |

#### Returns

[`AnnouncementBadgeResponse`](../../Common/type-aliases/AnnouncementBadgeResponse.md)

#### Inherited from

`Omit.announcementBadge`

***

### brandLogo?

> `optional` **brandLogo?**: `string`

Path to your app logo

Example:
Place file `logo.svg` to `./custom` folder and set this option:

```ts
brandLogo: '@@/logo.svg',
```

#### Inherited from

`Omit.brandLogo`

***

### brandName

> **brandName**: `string`

Your app name

#### Overrides

`Omit.brandName`

***

### brandNameSlug

> **brandNameSlug**: `string`

Slug which will be used on tech side e.g. to store cookies separately.
Created automatically from brandName if not set.

***

### customComponentsDir?

> `optional` **customComponentsDir?**: `string`

Relative or absolute path to custom components directory
By default equals  `./custom`.

Custom .vue files, images, and any other assets placed in this directory can be accessed in AdminForth components and configs with `@@/`.

For example if file path is `./custom/comp/my.vue`, you can use it in AdminForth config like this:

```ts
components: {
 show: '@@/comp/my.vue',
}
```

#### Inherited from

`Omit.customComponentsDir`

***

### customHeadItems?

> `optional` **customHeadItems?**: `object`[]

Allows adding custom elements (e.g., &lt;link&gt;, &lt;script&gt;, &lt;meta&gt;) to the &lt;head&gt; of the HTML document.
Each item must include a tag name and a set of attributes.

#### attributes

> **attributes**: `Record`\<`string`, `string` \| `boolean`\>

#### innerCode?

> `optional` **innerCode?**: `string`

#### tagName

> **tagName**: `string`

#### Overrides

`Omit.customHeadItems`

***

### customPages

> **customPages**: `AdminForthPageDeclaration`[]

Description of custom pages which will let register custom pages for custom routes in AdminForth.

#### Overrides

`Omit.customPages`

***

### dateFormats

> **dateFormats**: `string`

***

### datesFormat?

> `optional` **datesFormat?**: `string`

DayJS format string for all dates in the app.
Defaulted to 'MMM D, YYYY'

#### Inherited from

`Omit.datesFormat`

***

### emptyFieldPlaceholder?

> `optional` **emptyFieldPlaceholder?**: `string` \| \{ `list?`: `string`; `show?`: `string`; \}

Placeholder for empty fields in lists and show views, by default empty string ''

#### Inherited from

`Omit.emptyFieldPlaceholder`

***

### favicon?

> `optional` **favicon?**: `string`

Path to your app favicon

Example: 
Place file `favicon.png` to `./custom` folder and set this option:

```ts
favicon: '@@/favicon.png',
```

#### Inherited from

`Omit.favicon`

***

#### everyPageBottom

> `optional` **showBrandLogoInSidebar?**: `boolean`

Whether to show brand logo in sidebar
default is true

***

### showBrandNameInSidebar

> **showBrandNameInSidebar**: `boolean`

Whether to show brand name in sidebar
default is true

### singleTheme?

> `optional` **singleTheme?**: `"light"` \| `"dark"`

Whether to use single theme for the app

#### Inherited from

***

### styles?

> `optional` **styles?**: `Object`

Object to redefine default styles for AdminForth components.  Use this file as reference for all possible adjustments https://github.com/devforth/adminforth/blob/main/adminforth/modules/styles.ts

#### Inherited from

`Omit.styles`

***

### timeFormat

> **timeFormat**: `string`

DayJS format string for all datetimes in the app.
Defaulted to 'HH:mm:ss'

#### Overrides

`Omit.timeFormat`

***

### title?

> `optional` **title?**: `string`

HTML title tag value, defaults to brandName

#### Inherited from

`Omit.title`

***

### vueUsesFile?

> `optional` **vueUsesFile?**: `string`

Path to custom .ts file which allows to inject custom Vue uses in SPA or add custom imports.

Example: Create file: `./custom/vue-uses.ts` with next content:

```ts
import HighchartsVue from 'highcharts-vue';
// import '@@/custom.scss'; // here is how you can import custom styles

export default function (app) {
   app.use(HighchartsVue);
}
```

Install HighCharts into custom folder:

```bashcreating rec
cd custom
pnpm init -y
pnpm install highcharts highcharts-vue
```

And specify vueUsesFile in AdminForth config:

```ts
vueUsesFile: '@@/vue-uses.ts',
```

#### Inherited from

`Omit.vueUsesFile`

---

## AdminForthInputConfig


Main configuration object for AdminForth

## Properties

### auth?

> `optional` **auth?**: `object`

Authorization module configuration

#### adminUserAuthorize?

> `optional` **adminUserAuthorize?**: [`AdminUserAuthorizeFunction`](../type-aliases/AdminUserAuthorizeFunction.md) \| [`AdminUserAuthorizeFunction`](../type-aliases/AdminUserAuthorizeFunction.md)[]

Array of functions which will be called before any request to AdminForth API.

#### avatarUrl?

> `optional` **avatarUrl?**: (`adminUser`) => `string` \| `Promise`\<`string`\> \| `Promise`\<`undefined`\>

Function to return avatar URL for user.

##### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `adminUser` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) |

##### Returns

`string` \| `Promise`\<`string`\> \| `Promise`\<`undefined`\>

#### beforeLoginConfirmation?

> `optional` **beforeLoginConfirmation?**: [`BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction.md) \| [`BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeLoginConfirmationFunction.md)[]

Function or functions  which will be called before user try to login.
Each function will resive User object as an argument

#### clientIpHeader?

> `optional` **clientIpHeader?**: `string`

Client IP header name. If set, AdminForth will use this header to get client IP address.
Otherwise it will use first IP address from X-Forwarded-For header.
If you are using Cloudflare, set this to 'CF-Connecting-IP'. Case-insensitive.

#### demoCredentials?

> `optional` **demoCredentials?**: `string`

Pair of login and pass substitution for demo mode. Split by ':'
! This option is for demo purposes only, never use it for your projects

#### loginBackgroundImage?

> `optional` **loginBackgroundImage?**: `string`

File path to custom background image for login page
Example:
Place file `login-background.jpg` to `./custom` folder and set this option:

```ts
loginBackgroundImage: '@@/login-background.jpg',
```

#### loginBackgroundPosition?

> `optional` **loginBackgroundPosition?**: `"over"` \| `"1/2"` \| `"1/3"` \| `"2/3"` \| `"3/4"` \| `"2/5"` \| `"3/5"`

Position of background image on login page
'over' - image will be displayed over full login page under login form
'1/2' - image will be displayed on left 1/2 of login page

Default: '1/2'

#### loginPromptHTML?

> `optional` **loginPromptHTML?**: `string` \| (() => `string` \| `void` \| `Promise`\<`string` \| `void`\>)

Any prompt to show users on login. Supports HTML.

#### passwordHashField

> **passwordHashField**: `string`

Field name (column name) in user resource which will be used to get hash of password.
Can be e.g. 'passwordHash'

#### ~~rememberMeDays?~~

> `optional` **rememberMeDays?**: `number`

Old variable for remember me duration. Use rememberMeDuration instead.

##### Deprecated

Use rememberMeDuration instead.

#### rememberMeDuration?

> `optional` **rememberMeDuration?**: `string`

Remember me duration for "Remember Me" checkbox on login page.
If not set or set to null/undefined, "Remember Me" checkbox will not be displayed.
If rememberMeDuration is set, then users who check "Remember Me" will be staying logged in for this amount of time.
Format: "1s" (seconds), "1m" (minutes), "1h" (hours), or "1d" (days).
Example: "30d" for 30 days, "7d" for 7 days, "24h" for 24 hours.

#### removeBackgroundBlendMode?

> `optional` **removeBackgroundBlendMode?**: `boolean`

If true, background blend mode will be removed from login background image when position is 'over'

Default: false

#### resourceId?

> `optional` **resourceId?**: `string`

Legacy field left for backward compatibility. Use usersResourceId instead.

#### userFullNameField?

> `optional` **userFullNameField?**: `string`

Optionally if your users table has a field(column) with full name, you can set it here.
This field will be used to display user name in the top right corner of the admin panel.

#### userMenuSettingsPages?

> `optional` **userMenuSettingsPages?**: `object`[]

Add custom page to the settings page

#### usernameField

> **usernameField**: `string`

Field name (column name) in user resource which will be used as username for searching user in database during login.
Can be e.g. 'email' or 'username'

#### usersResourceId?

> `optional` **usersResourceId?**: `string`

Resource ID for resource which stores user table.
Resource is a table in database where users will be stored and fetched from. Resources and their ids are defined in resources section of the config.
In other words this setting is a reference to a table in database where users will be fetched from on login.

#### websocketSubscribed?

> `optional` **websocketSubscribed?**: (`topic`, `user`) => `void`

callback which will be called after user subscribes to websocket topic

##### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `topic` | `string` | topic on which user subscribed |
| `user` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) | user object |

##### Returns

`void`

#### websocketTopicAuth?

> `optional` **websocketTopicAuth?**: (`topic`, `user`) => `Promise`\<`boolean`\>

Can be used to limit user access when subscribing from frontend to websocket topics.

##### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| ------ | ------ | ------ |
| `topic` | `string` | topic where user is trying to subscribe |
| `user` | [`AdminUser`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminUser.md) | user object |

##### Returns

`Promise`\<`boolean`\>

- boolean, true if user is allowed to subscribe to this topic, false otherwise

***

### baseUrl?

> `optional` **baseUrl?**: `string`

If you want to Serve AdminForth from a subdirectory, e.g. on example.com/backoffice, you can specify it like:

```ts
baseUrl: '/backoffice',
```

***

### componentsToExplicitRegister?

> `optional` **componentsToExplicitRegister?**: [`AdminForthComponentDeclarationFull`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthComponentDeclarationFull.md)[]

Most of components are explicitely registered in AdminForth e.g. when you are using them in renderers, page injections and so on.
But some times you might want to have some components registered globally Explicitly. E.g. for ussage in other components without import.

```ts
componentsToExplicitRegister: [
 {
   file: '@@/my-component.vue',
   meta: {
     some: 'meta'
   }
 }
```

***

### customization?

> `optional` **customization?**: `AdminForthInputConfigCustomization`

Settings which allow you to customize AdminForth

***

### databaseConnectors?

> `optional` **databaseConnectors?**: `object`

If you want use custom DataSource which is not supported by AdminForth yet, you can define it's class here

#### Index Signature

\[`key`: `string`\]: [`IAdminForthDataSourceConnectorConstructor`](IAdminForthDataSourceConnectorConstructor.md)

***

### dataSources

> **dataSources**: [`AdminForthDataSource`](../type-aliases/AdminForthDataSource.md)[]

List of data sources which will be used to fetch data for resources.
Datasource is one database connection

***

### menu

> **menu**: [`AdminForthConfigMenuItem`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthConfigMenuItem.md)[]

Array of left sidebar menu items which will be displayed in the admin panel.
Menu items can be links to resources or custom pages.
Menu items can be grouped.

***

### resources

> **resources**: [`AdminForthResourceInput`](AdminForthResourceInput.md)[]

Array of resources which will be displayed in the admin panel.
Resource represents one table or collection in database.
Each resource has its own configuration.

---

## AdminForthResource


Resource describes one table or collection in database.
AdminForth generates set of pages for 'list', 'show', 'edit', 'create', 'filter' operations for each resource.

## Extends

- `Omit`\<[`AdminForthResourceInput`](AdminForthResourceInput.md), `"options"` \| `"columns"`\>

## Properties

### columns

> **columns**: [`AdminForthResourceColumn`](AdminForthResourceColumn.md)[]

***

### dataSource

> **dataSource**: `string`

ID of datasource which will be used to fetch data from.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`dataSource`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#datasource)

***

### dataSourceColumns

> **dataSourceColumns**: [`AdminForthResourceColumn`](AdminForthResourceColumn.md)[]

#### Overrides

[`AdminForthResourceInput`](AdminForthResourceInput.md).[`dataSourceColumns`](AdminForthResourceInput.md#datasourcecolumns)

***

### hooks?

> `optional` **hooks?**: `object`

Hooks allow you to change the data on different stages of resource lifecycle.
Hooks are functions which will be called on backend side (only backend side).

#### create?

> `optional` **create?**: `object`

##### create.afterSave?

> `optional` **afterSave?**: [`AfterCreateSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterCreateSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Initiate some trigger after record saved to database (e.g sync to another datasource)

##### create.beforeSave?

> `optional` **beforeSave?**: [`BeforeCreateSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeCreateSaveFunction.md)[]

Should return `ok: true` to continue saving pipeline and allow creating record in database, and `ok: false` to interrupt pipeline and prevent record creation.
If you need to show error on UI, set `error: \<error message\>` in response.

Typical use-cases:
- Create record by custom code (return `{ ok: false, newRecordId: <id of created record from custom code> }`)
- Validate record before saving to database and interrupt execution if validation failed (return `{ ok: false, error: <validation error> }`), though `allowedActions.create` should be preferred in most cases
- fill-in adminUser as creator of record (set `record.<some field> = x; return \{ ok: true \}`) 
- Attach additional data to record before saving to database (mostly fillOnCreate should be used instead)

#### delete?

> `optional` **delete?**: `object`

##### delete.afterSave?

> `optional` **afterSave?**: [`BeforeDeleteSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDeleteSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Initiate some trigger after record deleted from database (e.g sync to another datasource)

##### delete.beforeSave?

> `optional` **beforeSave?**: [`BeforeDeleteSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDeleteSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Validate that record can be deleted and interrupt execution if validation failed (`allowedActions.delete` should be preferred in most cases)

#### edit?

> `optional` **edit?**: `object`

##### edit.afterSave?

> `optional` **afterSave?**: [`AfterEditSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterEditSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Same as hooks.create.afterSave but for edit page

##### edit.beforeSave?

> `optional` **beforeSave?**: [`BeforeEditSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeEditSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Same as hooks.create.beforeSave but for edit page

#### list?

> `optional` **list?**: `object`

##### list.afterDatasourceResponse?

> `optional` **afterDatasourceResponse?**: [`AfterDataSourceResponseFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterDataSourceResponseFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Same as hooks.show.afterDatasourceResponse but applied for all records returned from database for 
showing in list view, e.g. add new field to each record in list view

##### list.beforeDatasourceRequest?

> `optional` **beforeDatasourceRequest?**: [`BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- add additional filters in addition to what user selected before fetching data from database.
- same as hooks.show.beforeDatasourceRequest

#### show?

> `optional` **show?**: `object`

##### show.afterDatasourceResponse?

> `optional` **afterDatasourceResponse?**: [`AfterDataSourceResponseFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterDataSourceResponseFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Transform value for some field for record returned from database before returning to frontend (minimize, sanitize, etc)
- If some-why you can't use `backendOnly` you can cleanup sensitive fields here
- Attach additional data to record before returning to frontend

##### show.beforeDatasourceRequest?

> `optional` **beforeDatasourceRequest?**: [`BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases: 
- request additional data from database before returning to frontend for soft-join

#### Overrides

`Omit.hooks`

***

### label

> **label**: `string`

Label for resource which will be displayed in the admin panel.
By default it equals to table name in database.

#### Overrides

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`label`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#label)

***

### options

> **options**: [`ResourceOptions`](ResourceOptions.md)

***

### plugins?

> `optional` **plugins?**: [`IAdminForthPlugin`](IAdminForthPlugin.md)[]

Array of plugins which will be used to modify resource configuration.

#### Overrides

[`AdminForthResourceInput`](AdminForthResourceInput.md).[`plugins`](AdminForthResourceInput.md#plugins)

***

### recordLabel

> **recordLabel**: (`record`) => `string`

Hook which allow you to modify record label

Example:

```ts
recordLabel: (record) => `${record.name} - ${record.id}`,
```

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `record` | `any` |

#### Returns

`string`

#### Overrides

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`recordLabel`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#recordlabel)

***

### resourceId

> **resourceId**: `string`

Unique identifier of resource. By default it equals to table name in database. 
If you wish you can explicitly set it to any string.
We added to support cases when 2 datasources have tables with the same name.

#### Overrides

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`resourceId`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#resourceid)

***

### table

> **table**: `string`

Table name in database which will be used to fetch data from. Might be case sensitive.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`table`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#table)

---

## AdminForthResourceColumn


## Extends

- `Omit`\<[`AdminForthResourceColumnCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md), `"showIn"` \| `"backendOnly"`\>

## Properties

### \_underlineType?

> `optional` **\_underlineType?**: `string`

Internal type which indicates original type of column in database.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md).[`_underlineType`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md#_underlinetype)

***

### allowMinMaxQuery?

> `optional` **allowMinMaxQuery?**: `boolean`

Allow AdminForth to execute SELECT min(column) and SELECT max(column) queries to get min and max values for this column.
This would improve UX of filters by adding sliders for numeric columns.

NOTE: By default is option is `false` to prevent performance issues on large tables.
If you are going to set it to `true`, make sure you have a one-item index on this column (one index for each column which has it) or ensure your table will not have a large number of records.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`allowMinMaxQuery`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#allowminmaxquery)

***

### allowModifyWhenNotShowInCreate?

> `optional` **allowModifyWhenNotShowInCreate?**: `boolean`

Allows setting this column on Create requests even when `showIn.create` resolves to `false`.

Default is `false`.

This is useful for custom create components which update hidden/technical fields via
`update:recordFieldValue`.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`allowModifyWhenNotShowInCreate`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#allowmodifywhennotshowincreate)

***

### allowModifyWhenNotShowInEdit?

> `optional` **allowModifyWhenNotShowInEdit?**: `boolean`

Allows updating this column on Edit requests even when `showIn.edit` resolves to `false`.

Default is `false`.

This is useful for custom edit components which update hidden/technical fields via
`update:recordFieldValue`.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`allowModifyWhenNotShowInEdit`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#allowmodifywhennotshowinedit)

***

### backendOnly?

> `optional` **backendOnly?**: [`BackendOnlyInput`](../type-aliases/BackendOnlyInput.md)

***

### components?

> `optional` **components?**: [`AdminForthFieldComponents`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthFieldComponents.md)

Custom components which will be used to render this field in the admin panel.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`components`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#components)

***

### editingNote?

> `optional` **editingNote?**: `object`

Whether AdminForth will show editing note near the field in edit/create form.

#### create?

> `optional` **create?**: `string`

#### edit?

> `optional` **edit?**: `string`

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md).[`editingNote`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md#editingnote)

***

### editReadonly?

> `optional` **editReadonly?**: `boolean`

Whether AdminForth will allow to edit this field in editing mode.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`editReadonly`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#editreadonly)

***

### enforceLowerCase?

> `optional` **enforceLowerCase?**: `boolean`

Will automatically convert any capital letters to lowercase in input during editing

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`enforceLowerCase`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#enforcelowercase)

***

### enum?

> `optional` **enum?**: [`AdminForthColumnEnumItem`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthColumnEnumItem.md)[]

Enum of possible values for this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`enum`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#enum)

***

### extra?

> `optional` **extra?**: `object`

An optional configuration object for extra settings.

#### jsonCollapsedLevel?

> `optional` **jsonCollapsedLevel?**: `number`

How many levels of JSON should be collapsed. 
`0` means - root level will be already collapsed e.g. `{a:1}` will show `{...}` where `'...'` is clickable
`1` means - root level will be shown, but next sub-level will be collapsed e.g. `{a: {b: 1}}` will show `{a: ...}` where `'...'` is clickable

Default is 1.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`extra`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#extra)

***

### fillOnCreate?

> `optional` **fillOnCreate?**: [`FillOnCreateFunction`](../../Common/type-aliases/FillOnCreateFunction.md)

Called on the backend when the record is saved to a database. Value returned by `fillOnCreate` will be saved to the database.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`fillOnCreate`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#filloncreate)

***

### filterOptions?

> `optional` **filterOptions?**: `object`

#### debounceTimeMs?

> `optional` **debounceTimeMs?**: `number`

Decrease number of requests by adding debounce time to filter requests.

#### multiselect?

> `optional` **multiselect?**: `boolean`

Boolean value that determines what select input type to display on filter page.

#### substringSearch?

> `optional` **substringSearch?**: `boolean`

If false - will force EQ operator for filter instead of ILIKE.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`filterOptions`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#filteroptions)

***

### foreignResource?

> `optional` **foreignResource?**: [`AdminForthForeignResource`](AdminForthForeignResource.md)

Foreign resource which has pk column with values same that written in this column.

#### Overrides

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`foreignResource`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#foreignresource)

***

### inputPrefix?

> `optional` **inputPrefix?**: `string`

Prefix and suffix for input field on create and edit pages.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`inputPrefix`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#inputprefix)

***

### inputSuffix?

> `optional` **inputSuffix?**: `string`

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`inputSuffix`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#inputsuffix)

***

### isArray?

> `optional` **isArray?**: `object`

Defines whether column is array and what type of items it contains.
AdminForth will use this information to render proper input fields in the admin panel with control buttons to add and remove items.
If enabled, requires column type to be JSON.
Cannot be used with masked columns, columns with foreignResource or primary key columns.

#### allowDuplicateItems?

> `optional` **allowDuplicateItems?**: `boolean`

If enabled, AdminForth will allow to add items with the same value.

#### enabled

> **enabled**: `boolean`

#### itemType

> **itemType**: [`AdminForthDataTypes`](../../Common/enumerations/AdminForthDataTypes.md)

Type of items in array. Cannot be JSON or RICHTEXT.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`isArray`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#isarray)

***

### isUnique?

> `optional` **isUnique?**: `boolean`

Whether AdminForth will request user to enter unique value during creating or editing record.
This option causes AdminForth to make a request to database to check if value is unique. 
(Constraints are not used, so for large-tables performance make sure you have unique index in database if you set this option to true)

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`isUnique`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#isunique)

***

### label?

> `optional` **label?**: `string`

How column can be labled in the admin panel.
Use it for renaming columns. Defaulted to column name with Uppercased first letter.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`label`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#label)

***

### listSticky?

> `optional` **listSticky?**: `boolean`

Sticky position for column

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`listSticky`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#liststicky)

***

### masked?

> `optional` **masked?**: `boolean`

Masked fields will be displayed as `*****` on Edit and Create pages.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`masked`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#masked)

***

### max?

> `optional` **max?**: `number`

Maximum value stored in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md).[`max`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md#max)

***

### maxLength?

> `optional` **maxLength?**: `number`

Maximum length of string that can be entered in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`maxLength`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#maxlength)

***

### maxValue?

> `optional` **maxValue?**: `number`

Maximum value that can be entered in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`maxValue`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#maxvalue)

***

### min?

> `optional` **min?**: `number`

Minimal value stored in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md).[`min`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md#min)

***

### minLength?

> `optional` **minLength?**: `number`

Minimum length of string that can be entered in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`minLength`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#minlength)

***

### minValue?

> `optional` **minValue?**: `number`

Minimum value that can be entered in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`minValue`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#minvalue)

***

### name

> **name**: `string`

Column name in database.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`name`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#name)

***

### primaryKey?

> `optional` **primaryKey?**: `boolean`

Whether to use this column as record identifier.
Only one column can be primary key.
AdminForth tries to guess primary key automatically first.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`primaryKey`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#primarykey)

***

### required?

> `optional` **required?**: `object`

Whether AdminForth will require this field to be filled in create and edit forms.
Can be set to boolean or object with create and edit properties.
If boolean, it will be used for both create and edit forms.

#### create?

> `optional` **create?**: `boolean`

#### edit?

> `optional` **edit?**: `boolean`

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md).[`required`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnCommon.md#required)

***

### showIf?

> `optional` **showIf?**: [`Predicate`](../../Common/type-aliases/Predicate.md)

Show field only if certain conditions are met.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`showIf`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#showif)

***

### showIn?

> `optional` **showIn?**: [`ShowIn`](../type-aliases/ShowIn.md)

***

### sortable?

> `optional` **sortable?**: `boolean`

Whether to allow this column to be sortable in list view.
If true, AdminForth will add sorting buttons to the column header in list view and clicking on it will change sorting state of the column.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`sortable`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#sortable)

***

### suggestOnCreate?

> `optional` **suggestOnCreate?**: `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `object` \| [`suggestOnCreateFunction`](../../Common/type-aliases/suggestOnCreateFunction.md)

Single value that will be substituted in create form. User can change it before saving the record.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`suggestOnCreate`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#suggestoncreate)

***

### type?

> `optional` **type?**: [`AdminForthDataTypes`](../../Common/enumerations/AdminForthDataTypes.md)

Type of data in column.
AdminForth will use this information to render proper input fields in the admin panel.
AdminForth tries to guess type of data from database column type automatically for typed databases like SQL-based.
However you can explicitly set it to any value. E.g. set AdminForthDataTypes.DATETIME for your string column in SQLite, which stores ISO date strings.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`type`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#type)

***

### validation?

> `optional` **validation?**: [`ValidationObject`](../../Common/type-aliases/ValidationObject.md)[]

Runtime validation Regexp rules for this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`validation`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#validation)

***

### virtual?

> `optional` **virtual?**: `boolean`

Allows to make the field which does not exist in database table.
Examples: add custom show field with user country flag:

```ts
{
 label: 'Country Flag',
 type: AdminForthDataTypes.STRING,
 virtual: true,
 showIn: {
   [AdminForthResourcePages.edit]: false,
   [AdminForthResourcePages.create]: false,
   [AdminForthResourcePages.filter]: false,
 },
 components: {
   show: '@@/CountryFlag.vue',
   list: '@@/CountryFlag.vue',
  },
}
```

This field will be displayed in show and list views with custom component `CountryFlag.vue`. CountryFlag.vue should be placed in custom folder and can be next:

```html
<template>
 {{ getFlagEmojiFromIso(record.ipCountry) }}
</template>

<script setup>
const props = defineProps(['record']);

function getFlagEmojiFromIso(iso) {
   return iso.toUpperCase().replace(/./g, (char) => String.fromCodePoint(char.charCodeAt(0) + 127397));
}
</script>
```

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`virtual`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#virtual)

---

## AdminForthResourceColumnInput


## Extends

- `Omit`\<[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md), `"showIn"` \| `"backendOnly"`\>

## Properties

### allowMinMaxQuery?

> `optional` **allowMinMaxQuery?**: `boolean`

Allow AdminForth to execute SELECT min(column) and SELECT max(column) queries to get min and max values for this column.
This would improve UX of filters by adding sliders for numeric columns.

NOTE: By default is option is `false` to prevent performance issues on large tables.
If you are going to set it to `true`, make sure you have a one-item index on this column (one index for each column which has it) or ensure your table will not have a large number of records.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`allowMinMaxQuery`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#allowminmaxquery)

***

### allowModifyWhenNotShowInCreate?

> `optional` **allowModifyWhenNotShowInCreate?**: `boolean`

Allows setting this column on Create requests even when `showIn.create` resolves to `false`.

Default is `false`.

This is useful for custom create components which update hidden/technical fields via
`update:recordFieldValue`.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`allowModifyWhenNotShowInCreate`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#allowmodifywhennotshowincreate)

***

### allowModifyWhenNotShowInEdit?

> `optional` **allowModifyWhenNotShowInEdit?**: `boolean`

Allows updating this column on Edit requests even when `showIn.edit` resolves to `false`.

Default is `false`.

This is useful for custom edit components which update hidden/technical fields via
`update:recordFieldValue`.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`allowModifyWhenNotShowInEdit`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#allowmodifywhennotshowinedit)

***

### backendOnly?

> `optional` **backendOnly?**: [`BackendOnlyInput`](../type-aliases/BackendOnlyInput.md)

***

### components?

> `optional` **components?**: [`AdminForthFieldComponents`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthFieldComponents.md)

Custom components which will be used to render this field in the admin panel.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`components`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#components)

***

### editingNote?

> `optional` **editingNote?**: `string` \| \{ `create?`: `string`; `edit?`: `string`; \}

Whether AdminForth will show editing note near the field in edit/create form.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`editingNote`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#editingnote)

***

### editReadonly?

> `optional` **editReadonly?**: `boolean`

Whether AdminForth will allow to edit this field in editing mode.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`editReadonly`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#editreadonly)

***

### enforceLowerCase?

> `optional` **enforceLowerCase?**: `boolean`

Will automatically convert any capital letters to lowercase in input during editing

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`enforceLowerCase`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#enforcelowercase)

***

### enum?

> `optional` **enum?**: [`AdminForthColumnEnumItem`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthColumnEnumItem.md)[]

Enum of possible values for this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`enum`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#enum)

***

### extra?

> `optional` **extra?**: `object`

An optional configuration object for extra settings.

#### jsonCollapsedLevel?

> `optional` **jsonCollapsedLevel?**: `number`

How many levels of JSON should be collapsed. 
`0` means - root level will be already collapsed e.g. `{a:1}` will show `{...}` where `'...'` is clickable
`1` means - root level will be shown, but next sub-level will be collapsed e.g. `{a: {b: 1}}` will show `{a: ...}` where `'...'` is clickable

Default is 1.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`extra`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#extra)

***

### fillOnCreate?

> `optional` **fillOnCreate?**: [`FillOnCreateFunction`](../../Common/type-aliases/FillOnCreateFunction.md)

Called on the backend when the record is saved to a database. Value returned by `fillOnCreate` will be saved to the database.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`fillOnCreate`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#filloncreate)

***

### filterOptions?

> `optional` **filterOptions?**: `object`

#### debounceTimeMs?

> `optional` **debounceTimeMs?**: `number`

Decrease number of requests by adding debounce time to filter requests.

#### multiselect?

> `optional` **multiselect?**: `boolean`

Boolean value that determines what select input type to display on filter page.

#### substringSearch?

> `optional` **substringSearch?**: `boolean`

If false - will force EQ operator for filter instead of ILIKE.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`filterOptions`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#filteroptions)

***

### foreignResource?

> `optional` **foreignResource?**: [`AdminForthForeignResource`](AdminForthForeignResource.md)

Foreign resource which has pk column with values same that written in this column.

#### Overrides

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`foreignResource`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#foreignresource)

***

### inputPrefix?

> `optional` **inputPrefix?**: `string`

Prefix and suffix for input field on create and edit pages.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`inputPrefix`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#inputprefix)

***

### inputSuffix?

> `optional` **inputSuffix?**: `string`

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`inputSuffix`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#inputsuffix)

***

### isArray?

> `optional` **isArray?**: `object`

Defines whether column is array and what type of items it contains.
AdminForth will use this information to render proper input fields in the admin panel with control buttons to add and remove items.
If enabled, requires column type to be JSON.
Cannot be used with masked columns, columns with foreignResource or primary key columns.

#### allowDuplicateItems?

> `optional` **allowDuplicateItems?**: `boolean`

If enabled, AdminForth will allow to add items with the same value.

#### enabled

> **enabled**: `boolean`

#### itemType

> **itemType**: [`AdminForthDataTypes`](../../Common/enumerations/AdminForthDataTypes.md)

Type of items in array. Cannot be JSON or RICHTEXT.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`isArray`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#isarray)

***

### isUnique?

> `optional` **isUnique?**: `boolean`

Whether AdminForth will request user to enter unique value during creating or editing record.
This option causes AdminForth to make a request to database to check if value is unique. 
(Constraints are not used, so for large-tables performance make sure you have unique index in database if you set this option to true)

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`isUnique`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#isunique)

***

### label?

> `optional` **label?**: `string`

How column can be labled in the admin panel.
Use it for renaming columns. Defaulted to column name with Uppercased first letter.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`label`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#label)

***

### listSticky?

> `optional` **listSticky?**: `boolean`

Sticky position for column

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`listSticky`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#liststicky)

***

### masked?

> `optional` **masked?**: `boolean`

Masked fields will be displayed as `*****` on Edit and Create pages.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`masked`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#masked)

***

### maxLength?

> `optional` **maxLength?**: `number`

Maximum length of string that can be entered in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`maxLength`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#maxlength)

***

### maxValue?

> `optional` **maxValue?**: `number`

Maximum value that can be entered in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`maxValue`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#maxvalue)

***

### minLength?

> `optional` **minLength?**: `number`

Minimum length of string that can be entered in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`minLength`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#minlength)

***

### minValue?

> `optional` **minValue?**: `number`

Minimum value that can be entered in this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`minValue`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#minvalue)

***

### name

> **name**: `string`

Column name in database.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`name`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#name)

***

### primaryKey?

> `optional` **primaryKey?**: `boolean`

Whether to use this column as record identifier.
Only one column can be primary key.
AdminForth tries to guess primary key automatically first.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`primaryKey`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#primarykey)

***

### required?

> `optional` **required?**: `boolean` \| \{ `create?`: `boolean`; `edit?`: `boolean`; \}

Whether AdminForth will require this field to be filled in create and edit forms.
Can be set to boolean or object with create and edit properties.
If boolean, it will be used for both create and edit forms.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`required`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#required)

***

### showIf?

> `optional` **showIf?**: [`Predicate`](../../Common/type-aliases/Predicate.md)

Show field only if certain conditions are met.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`showIf`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#showif)

***

### showIn?

> `optional` **showIn?**: [`ShowInInput`](../type-aliases/ShowInInput.md)

***

### sortable?

> `optional` **sortable?**: `boolean`

Whether to allow this column to be sortable in list view.
If true, AdminForth will add sorting buttons to the column header in list view and clicking on it will change sorting state of the column.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`sortable`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#sortable)

***

### suggestOnCreate?

> `optional` **suggestOnCreate?**: `string` \| `number` \| `boolean` \| `object` \| [`suggestOnCreateFunction`](../../Common/type-aliases/suggestOnCreateFunction.md)

Single value that will be substituted in create form. User can change it before saving the record.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`suggestOnCreate`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#suggestoncreate)

***

### type?

> `optional` **type?**: [`AdminForthDataTypes`](../../Common/enumerations/AdminForthDataTypes.md)

Type of data in column.
AdminForth will use this information to render proper input fields in the admin panel.
AdminForth tries to guess type of data from database column type automatically for typed databases like SQL-based.
However you can explicitly set it to any value. E.g. set AdminForthDataTypes.DATETIME for your string column in SQLite, which stores ISO date strings.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`type`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#type)

***

### validation?

> `optional` **validation?**: [`ValidationObject`](../../Common/type-aliases/ValidationObject.md)[]

Runtime validation Regexp rules for this field.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`validation`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#validation)

***

### virtual?

> `optional` **virtual?**: `boolean`

Allows to make the field which does not exist in database table.
Examples: add custom show field with user country flag:

```ts
{
 label: 'Country Flag',
 type: AdminForthDataTypes.STRING,
 virtual: true,
 showIn: {
   [AdminForthResourcePages.edit]: false,
   [AdminForthResourcePages.create]: false,
   [AdminForthResourcePages.filter]: false,
 },
 components: {
   show: '@@/CountryFlag.vue',
   list: '@@/CountryFlag.vue',
  },
}
```

This field will be displayed in show and list views with custom component `CountryFlag.vue`. CountryFlag.vue should be placed in custom folder and can be next:

```html
<template>
 {{ getFlagEmojiFromIso(record.ipCountry) }}
</template>

<script setup>
const props = defineProps(['record']);

function getFlagEmojiFromIso(iso) {
   return iso.toUpperCase().replace(/./g, (char) => String.fromCodePoint(char.charCodeAt(0) + 127397));
}
</script>
```

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md).[`virtual`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceColumnInputCommon.md#virtual)

---

## AdminForthResourceInput


## Extends

- `Omit`\<`NonNullable`\<[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md)\>, `"columns"` \| `"hooks"` \| `"options"`\>

## Properties

### columns

> **columns**: [`AdminForthResourceColumnInput`](AdminForthResourceColumnInput.md)[]

***

### dataSource

> **dataSource**: `string`

ID of datasource which will be used to fetch data from.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`dataSource`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#datasource)

***

### dataSourceColumns?

> `optional` **dataSourceColumns?**: [`AdminForthResourceColumn`](AdminForthResourceColumn.md)[]

***

### hooks?

> `optional` **hooks?**: `object`

Hooks allow you to change the data on different stages of resource lifecycle.
Hooks are functions which will be called on backend side (only backend side).

#### create?

> `optional` **create?**: `object`

##### create.afterSave?

> `optional` **afterSave?**: [`AfterCreateSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterCreateSaveFunction.md) \| [`AfterCreateSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterCreateSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Initiate some trigger after record saved to database (e.g sync to another datasource)

##### create.beforeSave?

> `optional` **beforeSave?**: [`BeforeCreateSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeCreateSaveFunction.md) \| [`BeforeCreateSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeCreateSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Validate record before saving to database and interrupt execution if validation failed (`allowedActions.create` should be preferred in most cases)
- fill-in adminUser as creator of record
- Attach additional data to record before saving to database (mostly fillOnCreate should be used instead)

#### delete?

> `optional` **delete?**: `object`

##### delete.afterSave?

> `optional` **afterSave?**: [`BeforeDeleteSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDeleteSaveFunction.md) \| [`BeforeDeleteSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDeleteSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Initiate some trigger after record deleted from database (e.g sync to another datasource)

##### delete.beforeSave?

> `optional` **beforeSave?**: [`BeforeDeleteSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDeleteSaveFunction.md) \| [`BeforeDeleteSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDeleteSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Validate that record can be deleted and interrupt execution if validation failed (`allowedActions.delete` should be preferred in most cases)

#### edit?

> `optional` **edit?**: `object`

##### edit.afterSave?

> `optional` **afterSave?**: [`AfterEditSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterEditSaveFunction.md) \| [`AfterEditSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterEditSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Same as hooks.create.afterSave but for edit page

##### edit.beforeSave?

> `optional` **beforeSave?**: [`BeforeEditSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeEditSaveFunction.md) \| [`BeforeEditSaveFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeEditSaveFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Same as hooks.create.beforeSave but for edit page

#### list?

> `optional` **list?**: `object`

##### list.afterDatasourceResponse?

> `optional` **afterDatasourceResponse?**: [`AfterDataSourceResponseFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterDataSourceResponseFunction.md) \| [`AfterDataSourceResponseFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterDataSourceResponseFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Same as hooks.show.afterDatasourceResponse but applied for all records returned from database for 
showing in list view, e.g. add new field to each record in list view

##### list.beforeDatasourceRequest?

> `optional` **beforeDatasourceRequest?**: [`BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction.md) \| [`BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- add additional filters in addition to what user selected before fetching data from database.
- same as hooks.show.beforeDatasourceRequest

#### show?

> `optional` **show?**: `object`

##### show.afterDatasourceResponse?

> `optional` **afterDatasourceResponse?**: [`AfterDataSourceResponseFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterDataSourceResponseFunction.md) \| [`AfterDataSourceResponseFunction`](../type-aliases/AfterDataSourceResponseFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases:
- Transform value for some field for record returned from database before returning to frontend (minimize, sanitize, etc)
- If some-why you can't use `backendOnly` you can cleanup sensitive fields here
- Attach additional data to record before returning to frontend

##### show.beforeDatasourceRequest?

> `optional` **beforeDatasourceRequest?**: [`BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction.md) \| [`BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction`](../type-aliases/BeforeDataSourceRequestFunction.md)[]

Typical use-cases: 
- request additional data from database before returning to frontend for soft-join

***

### label?

> `optional` **label?**: `string`

Label for resource which will be displayed in the admin panel.
By default it equals to table name in database.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`label`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#label)

***

### options?

> `optional` **options?**: [`ResourceOptionsInput`](ResourceOptionsInput.md)

***

### plugins?

> `optional` **plugins?**: [`IAdminForthPlugin`](IAdminForthPlugin.md)[]

Array of plugins which will be used to modify resource configuration.

***

### recordLabel?

> `optional` **recordLabel?**: (`item`) => `string`

Hook which allow you to modify record label

Example:

```ts
recordLabel: (record) => `${record.name} - ${record.id}`,
```

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `item` | `any` |

#### Returns

`string`

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`recordLabel`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#recordlabel)

***

### resourceId?

> `optional` **resourceId?**: `string`

Unique identifier of resource. By default it equals to table name in database. 
If you wish you can explicitly set it to any string.
We added to support cases when 2 datasources have tables with the same name.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`resourceId`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#resourceid)

***

### table

> **table**: `string`

Table name in database which will be used to fetch data from. Might be case sensitive.

#### Inherited from

[`AdminForthResourceInputCommon`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md).[`table`](../../Common/interfaces/AdminForthResourceInputCommon.md#table)
